---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Cooper Joseph Sharp"
date: "2026-03-16"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(car)         # vif()
library(broom)       # tidy()
library(pscl)        # pR2()
library(lmtest)      # lrtest(), coeftest()
library(sandwich)    # vcovHC(), vcovCL() if needed

```

```{r}

setwd("~/Documents/GitHub/2025-ICRG")
df     <- read.csv("derivatives/scoring/scoring-mastersheet.csv")
df_geo <- read.csv("derivatives/scoring/geo-variables.csv")

```

```{r Create Reward Sens. Composite}

# Composite BIS/BAS score
df$bisbas_bas <- df$bisbas_drive + df$bisbas_fun_seek + df$bisbas_reward_res

# Calculate z-scores
calculate_z <- function(X, X_mean, S) {
  return((X - X_mean) / S)
}

df$bisbas_bas_z_score <- calculate_z(
  df$bisbas_bas,
  mean(df$bisbas_bas, na.rm = TRUE),
  sd(df$bisbas_bas, na.rm = TRUE))

df$spsrq_sr_z_score <- calculate_z(
  df$spsrq_sr,
  mean(df$spsrq_sr, na.rm = TRUE),
  sd(df$spsrq_sr, na.rm = TRUE))

# Relative sensitivity: BISBAS - SPSRQ
df$rs_standard <- df$bisbas_bas_z_score - df$spsrq_sr_z_score

# Remove outliers in rs_standard
q3 <- quantile(df$rs_standard, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
q1 <- quantile(df$rs_standard, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
iqr <- q3 - q1
upper_bound <- q3 + 1.5 * iqr
lower_bound <- q1 - 1.5 * iqr
df <- df[!(df$rs_standard >= upper_bound | df$rs_standard <= lower_bound) | is.na(df$rs_standard), ]

# Composite reward score
df$rew_composite <- df$spsrq_sr_z_score + df$bisbas_bas_z_score

```

```{r}
# Pull Gini index by ZIP
gini_zip <- get_acs(
  geography = "zcta",
  variables = c(gini = "B19083_001"),
  year = 2021,
  survey = "acs5") %>%
  dplyr::select(GEOID, estimate) %>%
  rename(zipcode = GEOID, gini = estimate)

# Join Gini data
df <- df %>%
  mutate(zipcode = as.character(demo_zip_prim)) %>%
  left_join(gini_zip, by = "zipcode")

df <- df %>%
  left_join(df_geo, by = "ResponseId") %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ ifelse(. == -999, NA, .))) %>%
  filter(if_all(c(sogs_total, bbgs_total, mandf_casinog, mandf_onlineg,
                  TotalCrime, unemployed, gini, demo_yrs, demo_gender,
                  rew_composite, susd_depression, susd_mania,
                  scared_total, uclal_total), ~ !is.na(.)))
```

```{r Clean and Label Data}

n_before <- nrow(df)

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    pg = case_when(
      # Group 2: Gambles and meets problem gambling criteria
      sogs_total >= 1 & bbgs_total >= 4 & (mandf_casinog + mandf_onlineg) >= 1 ~ 2,
      # Group 0: Non-gamblers
      sogs_total == 0 & bbgs_total <= 3 & (mandf_casinog + mandf_onlineg) == 0 ~ 0,
      # Group 1: Gambles but does not meet problem gambling criteria
      TRUE ~ 1
    ),
    
    age_group = cut(
      demo_yrs,
      breaks = c(17, 25, 35, 45, 55, 65, 80),
      labels = c("18–25", "26–35", "36–45", "46–55", "56–65", "66–80")
    ),
    
    gender = factor(
      demo_gender,
      levels = c(1, 2),
      labels = c("Male", "Female")
    ),
    
    income = factor(
      ses_tpi,
      levels = 1:7,
      labels = c("$0 to $25,000", "$26,000 to $50,000", "$51,000-$75,000",
                 "$76,000-$100,000", "$101,000-$150,000", "$151,000-$200,000", "$200,000 or more")
    )
  )

n_after <- nrow(df)
cat("Total retained:", n_after, "\n")
cat("Group 0 (Non-gamblers):", sum(df$pg == 0), "\n")
cat("Group 2 (Gamblers, no PG criteria):", sum(df$pg == 2), "\n")
cat("Group 1 (Problem gamblers):", sum(df$pg == 1), "\n")

table(df$gender, df$pg)

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    income_median_split = factor(
      ifelse(ses_tpi <= 2, "Below Median (<$40k)", "Above Median (≥$40k)"),
      levels = c("Below Median (<$40k)", "Above Median (≥$40k)")
    )
  )

```

```{r}

# ── Colours ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
col_ng  <- "pink3"   # Non-Gambler  (light blue)
col_rg  <- "purple1"   # Recreational (mid blue)
col_pg  <- "purple4"   # Problem      (dark blue)

group_colours <- c("Non-Gambler" = col_ng,
                   "Recreational" = col_rg,
                   "Problem Gambler" = col_pg)

# ── Helper: add Status factor ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
add_status <- function(data) {
  data %>%
    mutate(Status = factor(
      case_when(
        pg == 0 ~ "Non-Gambler",
        pg == 1 ~ "Porblem Gambler",
        pg == 2 ~ "Recreational"
      ),
      levels = c("Non-Gambler", "Problem Gambler", "Recreational")
    ))
}

# Apply globally
df <- add_status(df)

```

```{r}

dat <- df %>%
  filter(pg != 2) %>%
  
  # ── Drop NAs on all variables used downstream ──────────────────────────────
  drop_na(
    pg, demo_gender, income_median_split,
    TotalCrime, unemployed, gini,
    demo_yrs,
    rew_composite,
    dospert_gam, dospert_soc, dospert_inv,
    dospert_hs, dospert_ethic, dospert_rec,
    susd_depression, susd_mania, scared_total,
    uclal_total, bisbas_bas, bisbas_bis, sas_total
  ) %>%
  
  mutate(
    PG     = as.integer(pg),
    gender = factor(demo_gender),
    income = factor(income_median_split),
    
    # Neighbourhood disadvantage
    z_crime  = as.numeric(scale(TotalCrime)),
    z_unemp  = as.numeric(scale(unemployed)),
    z_gini   = as.numeric(scale(gini)),
    
    # Demographics
    z_age    = as.numeric(scale(demo_yrs)),
    
    # Psychological
    z_reward        = as.numeric(scale(rew_composite)),
    z_dospert       = as.numeric(scale(dospert_gam + dospert_soc + dospert_inv + dospert_hs + dospert_ethic + dospert_rec)),
    z_dospert_inv   = as.numeric(scale(dospert_inv)),
    z_dospert_gam   = as.numeric(scale(dospert_gam)),
    z_dospert_ethic = as.numeric(scale(dospert_ethic)),
    z_dospert_soc   = as.numeric(scale(dospert_soc)),
    z_dospert_hs    = as.numeric(scale(dospert_hs)),
    z_dospert_rec   = as.numeric(scale(dospert_rec)),
    z_depression    = as.numeric(scale(susd_depression)),
    z_mania         = as.numeric(scale(susd_mania)),
    z_anxiety       = as.numeric(scale(scared_total)),
    z_loneliness    = as.numeric(scale(uclal_total)),
    z_bisbas_bas    = as.numeric(scale(bisbas_bas)),
    z_bisbas_bis    = as.numeric(scale(bisbas_bis)),
    z_sas_total     = as.numeric(scale(sas_total)),
    
    # Neighbourhood disadvantage composite
    z_neigh_disadv = rowMeans(cbind(
      as.numeric(scale(TotalCrime)),
      as.numeric(scale(unemployed)),
      as.numeric(scale(gini))
    ), na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  
  # ── Remove gender == 3 (no NA ambiguity after drop_na above) ───────────────
  filter(gender %in% c(1, 2))

# Checks
table(dat$PG, useNA = "ifany")
table(dat$gender, useNA = "ifany")
cat("Analytic N =", nrow(dat), "\n")

```

```{r}

# ── Hierarchical logistic models ──────────────────────────────────────────────

# Block 1: Demographics + neighbourhood context
m1 <- glm(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + income,
  data = dat, family = binomial(link = "logit"))

# Block 2: Add reward sensitivity + risk-taking
m2 <- glm(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + income +
    z_reward + z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic + z_dospert_gam + z_dospert_inv + z_dospert_rec,
  data = dat, family = binomial(link = "logit"))

# Block 3: Add clinical/social symptoms
m3 <- glm(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + income +
    z_reward + z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic + z_dospert_gam + z_dospert_inv + z_dospert_rec +
    z_depression + z_mania + z_anxiety + z_loneliness,
  data = dat, family = binomial(link = "logit"))

# Block 4: Add interactions
m4 <- glm(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + income +
    z_reward + z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic + z_dospert_gam + z_dospert_inv + z_dospert_rec +
    z_depression + z_mania + z_anxiety + z_loneliness +
    z_reward:z_loneliness +
    z_reward:z_age,
  data = dat, family = binomial(link = "logit"))

```

```{r}

# ── Model comparisons ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
anova(m1, m2, test = "Chisq")
anova(m2, m3, test = "Chisq")
anova(m3, m4, test = "Chisq")

AIC(m1, m2, m3, m4)
BIC(m1, m2, m3, m4)

pR2(m1); pR2(m2); pR2(m3); pR2(m4)

```

```{r}

# ── Odds ratios ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
get_or_table <- function(model) {
  est <- coef(model)
  ci  <- suppressMessages(confint(model))
  data.frame(
    term    = names(est),
    OR      = exp(est),
    CI_low  = exp(ci[, 1]),
    CI_high = exp(ci[, 2]),
    row.names = NULL
  )
}

or_m1 <- get_or_table(m1)
or_m2 <- get_or_table(m2)
or_m3 <- get_or_table(m3)
or_m4 <- get_or_table(m4)
or_m4

tidy(m4)

```

```{r}
# ── Multicollinearity ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
vif(m3)
vif(m4)

```

```{r}

# ── Robust SEs ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
coeftest(m4, vcov = vcovHC(m4, type = "HC3"))

# Cluster-robust by ZIP code if needed:
# coeftest(m4, vcov = vcovCL(m4, cluster = ~ demo_zip_prim))

```

```{r}

# ── Predicted probabilities: reward × loneliness interaction ─────────────────
newdat <- expand.grid(
  z_neigh_disadv = 0,
  gender         = levels(dat$gender)[1],
  income         = levels(dat$income)[1],
  z_age          = 0,
  z_sas_total    = 0, 
  z_reward       = c(-1, 0, 1),
  z_dospert      = 0,
  z_depression   = 0,
  z_mania        = 0,
  z_anxiety      = 0,
  z_loneliness   = c(-1, 0, 1)
)

newdat$pred_prob <- predict(m4, newdata = newdat, type = "response")
newdat

```

```{r}

or_m4 %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(term = reorder(term, OR)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(OR, term)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = CI_low, xmax = CI_high), height = 0.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3, colour = col_pg) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(x = "Odds Ratio (log scale)", y = NULL,
       title = "Odds Ratios from Final Model (m4)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

```

```{r}

# ── 2. Clean Coefficient Table ────────────────────────────────────────────────
# p-values, ORs, and CIs in one readable table
library(knitr)

tidy(m4, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(across(c(estimate, conf.low, conf.high), ~ round(., 3)),
         p.value = round(p.value, 3),
         sig = case_when(
           p.value < .001 ~ "***",
           p.value < .01  ~ "**",
           p.value < .05  ~ "*",
           p.value < .1   ~ ".",
           TRUE           ~ ""
         )) %>%
  rename(OR = estimate, CI_low = conf.low, CI_high = conf.high) %>%
  kable()

```

```{r}

# ── 3. Model Fit Summary ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# McFadden R2 and AIC side by side
data.frame(
  Model        = c("M1: Context", "M2: + Reward/Risk", 
                   "M3: + Clinical", "M4: + Interactions"),
  AIC          = round(AIC(m1, m2, m3, m4)$AIC, 1),
  BIC          = round(BIC(m1, m2, m3, m4)$BIC, 1),
  McFadden_R2  = round(c(pR2(m1)["McFadden"], pR2(m2)["McFadden"],
                          pR2(m3)["McFadden"], pR2(m4)["McFadden"]), 3)
) %>% kable()

```

```{r}

# ── 4. Predicted Probability Plots ───────────────────────────────────────────
# More intuitive than log-odds — shows actual probability of being PG

# Reward × Loneliness interaction
newdat %>%
  mutate(
    Loneliness = factor(z_loneliness, labels = c("Low (−1SD)", "Mean", "High (+1SD)")),
    Reward     = factor(z_reward,     labels = c("Low (−1SD)", "Mean", "High (+1SD)"))
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(Reward, pred_prob, colour = Loneliness, group = Loneliness)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_line() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent, limits = c(0, 1)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(col_ng, col_rg, col_pg)) +
  labs(x = "Reward Sensitivity", y = "Predicted P(Problem Gambler)",
       colour = "Loneliness") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

```

```{r}

# ── 5. Confusion Matrix / Classification Accuracy ────────────────────────────
# Shows how well the model predicts group membership
predicted_class <- ifelse(predict(m4, type = "response") > 0.5, 1, 0)
table(Predicted = predicted_class, Actual = dat$PG)

# Overall accuracy
mean(predicted_class == dat$PG, na.rm = TRUE)

```
